story.hook: Rare phenomena shows why the aurora glow matters.
story.lead: Half the moon glows every night, even when we can't see it. The sun lights the far side too, we just never catch it. That's why the dark part still gets a faint glow from Earthshine. Look close—you can see the moon's outline in the shadow. Pretty cool that our own clouds bounce light back to light the moon.
